2021-03-01.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-03-01.abstract: Protests against Spain’s biting economic crisis.

2021-03-01.title: Behind the Lines of Britain’s Covid War
2021-03-01.abstract: The government has laid out plans for gradual reopening. But in cramped intensive care wards, teeming with patients and doctors near despair, the battle is unrelenting.

2021-03-01.title: Thousands of farmworkers are prioritized for vaccinations.
2021-03-01.abstract: The desert valley tucked behind the San Jacinto Mountains in California is best known for the Coachella music festival and a series of lush resort towns where well-heeled snowbirds go to golf, sunbathe and party.

2021-03-01.title: In a packed intensive care unit, here’s a glimpse behind the lines of Britain’s Covid war.
2021-03-01.abstract: Britain often tried to ignore the coronavirus as it advanced from Asia to northern Italy to the United States, leapfrogging political and geographic boundaries, carried by airline passengers and cruise ship vacationers just as the government entered the final phase of the departure from the European Union.

2021-03-01.title: The Philippines begins its inoculation campaign, but public distrust runs deep.
2021-03-01.abstract: MANILA — The Philippines, which has had one of the worst coronavirus outbreaks in Southeast Asia, began its inoculation campaign on Monday even as the government struggled to reassure a population wary of foreign-made vaccines.

2021-03-02.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in March
2021-03-02.abstract: Our streaming picks for March, including ‘Biggie: I Got a Story to Tell,’ ‘Bill & Ted Face the Music’ and ‘Coming 2 America’

2021-03-02.title: Hundreds of Girls Abducted From Nigerian School Are Freed, Official Says
2021-03-02.abstract: It was the second time in less than a week that gunmen in the country had released kidnapped schoolchildren.

2021-03-02.title: Criminal Inquiries Loom Over al-Assad’s Use of Chemical Arms in Syria
2021-03-02.abstract: Investigations in France and Germany could lead to prosecutions of President Bashar al-Assad and members of his upper echelon over one of the Syrian war’s signature atrocities.

2021-03-02.title: Defective Birth Control Blamed for Scores of Unplanned Pregnancies in Chile
2021-03-02.abstract: The public health system delivered, and then quietly recalled, 276,890 potentially flawed packets of birth control pills. At least 140 women believe they got pregnant because of the error.

2021-03-03.title: Dolly Parton, who helped fund the Moderna vaccine, gets a ‘dose of her own medicine.’
2021-03-03.abstract: The country music star broke into song before she was inoculated, and urged her fans to “get out there and get your shot.”

2021-03-03.title: Your Wednesday Briefing
2021-03-03.abstract: The ethical quandary of vaccine passports.

2021-03-03.title: Without Backpackers to Pick Them, Crops Rot by the Ton in Australia
2021-03-03.abstract: The pandemic has exposed the shaky labor foundation of the country’s agriculture industry, spurring calls for an immigration overhaul.

2021-03-03.title: Videos Show Extent of Myanmar Military’s Bloody Crackdown
2021-03-03.abstract: A trove of visual evidence reveals how soldiers and the police used rifles and other lethal weapons against anti-coup protests across the country.

2021-03-03.title: Brazil’s Covid Crisis Is a Warning to the Whole World, Scientists Say
2021-03-03.abstract: Brazil is seeing a record number of deaths, and the spread of a more contagious coronavirus variant that may cause reinfection.

2021-03-04.title: Transgender Woman Expelled From South Korean Army Is Found Dead
2021-03-04.abstract: Byeon Hee-su, who was declared unfit to serve last year after her gender-reassignment surgery, had sued to be reinstated.

2021-03-04.title: Pope’s Planned Visit to Iraq, Amid Pandemic, Raises Questions of Timing
2021-03-04.abstract: Pope Francis has said he is determined to visit the war-torn country. But some worry that the visit will become a superspreader event in a land where the virus still rages.

2021-03-04.title: The E.U.’s drug regulator has begun a formal review of Russia’s Sputnik V shots.
2021-03-04.abstract: The European Union drug regulator announced on Thursday that it was beginning a rolling review of the Russian-developed Sputnik V vaccine, after one of the bloc’s members moved unilaterally to use the shots and another is about to do the same.

2021-03-04.title: After India Jabs Millions, Its Covid-19 Vaccine Shows Potency
2021-03-04.abstract: The Indian maker of Covaxin says interim results from clinical trials suggest it is safe and effective, potentially removing one hurdle to New Delhi’s ambitious inoculation campaign.

2021-03-05.title: A Confident China Promises Robust Growth and a Hard Line on Hong Kong
2021-03-05.abstract: Officials said the economy would grow by more than 6 percent this year. They also pushed ahead with an electoral overhaul in Hong Kong that would cripple the opposition.

2021-03-05.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-03-05.abstract: The female heroes of Myanmar’s protests.

2021-03-05.title: As a Doctor, I Was Skeptical About the Covid Vaccine. Then I Reviewed the Science.
2021-03-05.abstract: How one emergency department doctor shifted her views.

2021-03-05.title: China subjects some travelers to anal swabs, angering foreign governments.
2021-03-05.abstract: Some experts have questioned the need for the tests, saying that nasal and throat swabs are still the most effective because the coronavirus is contracted through the respiratory tract.

2021-03-05.title: In Iraq’s Christian Heartland, a Feud Over a Town’s Identity
2021-03-05.abstract: Pope Francis will arrive in a country whose ancient Christian towns are in danger of disappearing. In one, a Christian community’s survival may come at the expense of another minority group.

2021-03-06.title: Anger at Coronavirus Response Boils Over in Paraguay
2021-03-06.abstract: The police in the Paraguayan capital, Asunción, fired tear gas and rubber bullets on Friday to disperse crowds protesting record numbers of coronavirus cases and the country’s troubled health care system.

2021-03-06.title: In a Land Dominated by Ex-Rebels, Kosovo Women Find Power at the Ballot Box
2021-03-06.abstract: Women are winning greater political representation in Kosovo, raising hopes of more equality in a country still living with the scars of the war against Serbian rule in the 1990s.

2021-03-06.title: After Coup in Myanmar, a Career Diplomat Takes a Stand
2021-03-06.abstract: At the United Nations, U Kyaw Moe Tun declared his new military masters illegitimate. They fired him, but he has no intention of leaving.

2021-03-06.title: In Hong Kong, Foreign Tourists Are Replaced by a Local Variety
2021-03-06.abstract: Bored and trapped in an area one-third the size of Rhode Island, Hong Kongers have sought out the most far-flung corners of their city, mobbing parklands with crowds typically seen downtown.

2021-03-06.title: As Covid Deaths Soar in Brazil, Bolsonaro Hails an Untested Nasal Spray
2021-03-06.abstract: Vaccines and critical-care beds are in short supply. Yet President Jair Bolsonaro is railing against social distancing and calling an experimental treatment from Israel “miraculous.”

2021-03-07.title: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe Is Freed by Iran but Not Yet Cleared to Return to U.K.
2021-03-07.abstract: The British-Iranian woman has been detained in Tehran for five years on charges of plotting to overthrow the government, accusations that her family and supporters say are baseless.

2021-03-07.title: Abortion Is Now Legal in Argentina, but Opponents Are Making It Hard to Get
2021-03-07.abstract: Anti-abortion activists are suing to block a new law allowing the procedure, and many doctors in conservative areas have declared themselves conscientious objectors.

2021-03-07.title: Philippine Security Forces Are Accused of Killing 9 Activists
2021-03-07.abstract: A rights group said the activists were killed in coordinated raids in four provinces. An official confirmed that nine people were dead.

2021-03-07.title: Israel reopens to fully vaccinated ‘Green Pass’ holders — nearly half of the population.
2021-03-07.abstract: JERUSALEM — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel enjoyed a cappuccino and cake on the terrace of a Jerusalem cafe on Sunday morning to mark the broadest reopening of the country’s economy since the first coronavirus lockdown began a year ago.

2021-03-07.title: The U.S. risks a new surge as variants spread and restrictions are relaxed, Fauci said.
2021-03-07.abstract: The decline in coronavirus infections and deaths has stalled, scientists warned, even as new variants are spreading.

2021-03-08.title: It’s a girl: Meghan and Harry reveal that Archie will have a sister.
2021-03-08.abstract: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle used the occasion of the Oprah interview to tell the world their second child will be a girl.

2021-03-08.title: ‘I Just Didn’t Want to Be Alive Anymore’: Meghan Says Life as Royal Made Her Suicidal
2021-03-08.abstract: In a bombshell interview with Oprah Winfrey, the Duchess of Sussex said she had asked officials at Buckingham Palace for medical help but was told it would damage the institution.

2021-03-08.title: Meghan and Harry recount conversations with royals about their son’s skin tone.
2021-03-08.abstract: Meghan told Oprah that there were “concerns and conversations about how dark his skin might be.”

2021-03-08.title: Meghan says she sought help from the palace for suicidal thoughts, but was rebuffed.
2021-03-08.abstract: In an emotional disclosure, Meghan Markle described suffering persistent suicidal thoughts during her pregnancy after bullying from the news media and being barred from leaving the house.

2021-03-08.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-03-08.abstract: Israel reopens to its vaccinated populace.

2021-03-09.title: A Tale Old and New as Another Royal Wife Breaks Free
2021-03-09.abstract: A quarter-century after Princess Diana broke her silence about life among the British royals, Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, did the same. Their stories were remarkably similar.

2021-03-09.title: Millions of Students in England Go Back to School
2021-03-09.abstract: Students aged 5 to 11 returned to school on Monday for the first time since January, as the country’s lockdown eases. Older students will return in the coming weeks.

2021-03-09.title: Israel Begins Vaccinations for Palestinian Workers
2021-03-09.abstract: Hundreds of Palestinians with permits to work in Israel lined up for coronavirus vaccinations at checkpoints along the border with the West Bank on Monday.

2021-03-09.title: Black Britons Empathize With Meghan After TV Interview
2021-03-09.abstract: For many Black Britons, Prince Harry and Meghan’s television interview with Oprah Winfrey on Sunday reaffirmed their belief that racism played a role in the couple’s decision to leave the country.

2021-03-10.title: Piers Morgan Leaves ‘Good Morning Britain’ After Attacks on Meghan
2021-03-10.abstract: The television anchor stormed off the set of an ITV news program after his co-host, Alex Beresford, criticized his remarks about the Duchess of Sussex.

2021-03-10.title: In Australia, Hollywood Stars Have Found an Escape From the Virus. Who’s Jealous?
2021-03-10.abstract: Dozens of international film productions have been lured to the country, where cases of the coronavirus are few. In turn, actors have found almost paradise.

2021-03-10.title: ‘There’s No Town Left’: Fukushima’s Eerie Landscapes
2021-03-10.abstract: Ten years after a devastating earthquake and tsunami led to a nuclear meltdown in northern Japan, residents are readjusting to places that feel familiar and hostile at once.

2021-03-10.title: The key to President Biden’s successful vaccination campaign? Underpromise and overdeliver.
2021-03-10.abstract: When President Biden pledged last week to amass enough shots by late May to inoculate every American adult, the pronouncement was greeted as a triumphant acceleration of a vaccination campaign that seemed only weeks earlier to be faltering.

2021-03-11.title: Mexico Set to Legalize Marijuana, Becoming World’s Largest Market
2021-03-11.abstract: Lawmakers in Mexico have approved a bill to legalize recreational cannabis, but in a country still marred by a deadly drug war, the proposal has proved divisive.

2021-03-11.abstract: The brakes on a bus carrying 66 students, teachers and relatives who had visited an Islamic missionary’s tomb appear to have malfunctioned, killing 29.

2021-03-11.title: A Village Erased
2021-03-11.abstract: The earthquake and tsunami of March 11, 2011, wiped away the ancient Japanese village of Kesen. In the past decade, a small group of survivors has valiantly tried to rebuild the community, but a grim reality has set in: This emptiness will last forever.

2021-03-11.title: A growing number of U.S. states are expanding vaccine eligibility.
2021-03-11.abstract: Alaska, Georgia, Minnesota, New York, Ohio and Texas are among those offering shots to more people amid an energized national inoculation campaign.

2021-03-12.abstract: Millions of people have received the vaccine without safety problems, and there is no evidence of any causal link between the vaccine and blood clots.

2021-03-12.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-03-12.abstract: A fourth vaccine approved by the E.U.

2021-03-12.title: Hungary pays big for a Chinese vaccine.
2021-03-12.abstract: The European nation has agreed to pay $36 per dose of the Sinopharm vaccine, according to contracts put online by a Hungarian official.

2021-03-12.title: Trump as You’ve Never Seen Him Before
2021-03-12.abstract: A furniture maker and decorator in China created a stir — and inspired copycats — by casting a ceramic sculpture of the former president in a meditative pose that evokes the Buddha.

2021-03-12.abstract: A wave of responses from women who have experienced depression and suicidal thoughts is shedding light on the stark differences between how pregnancy is perceived and lived.

2021-03-13.title: China asks visa applicants to get inoculated with Chinese-made vaccines.
2021-03-13.abstract: A new rule for foreigners applying in Hong Kong for visas gives preference to those who choose shots produced in China.

2021-03-13.abstract: The island has used its pandemic success to sell something scarce: life without fear of the coronavirus. Citizens have flocked home from abroad, helping to fuel an economic boom.

2021-03-13.abstract: In the southern city of Perpignan, voters who had long built a dam against the far right turned in the last election. Some wonder whether it’s a harbinger of things to come.

2021-03-13.title: Can the World Learn From South Africa’s Vaccine Trials?
2021-03-13.abstract: Vaccine trials are often done in wealthier countries. Scientists say the South Africa experience proves the value of trials in the global south.

2021-03-13.abstract: In Afghanistan, a rise in targeted attacks that the government seems unable to stop has also encouraged the settling of old scores through vendetta killings.

2021-03-14.title: Germany Launches Election Year With Losses for Merkel’s Party in Two States
2021-03-14.abstract: Voters choosing regional governments in southwestern states punished the conservative party of Chancellor Angela Merkel after a series of scandals.

2021-03-14.abstract: The undercount complicates President Biden’s decision on carrying out a complete withdrawal by May 1, as his administration tries to jump-start peace talks.

2021-03-14.abstract: At least 51 people were fatally shot over the weekend, but the nationwide protest movement shows no sign of waning.

2021-03-14.abstract: More payments will be sent out in the next few weeks, with some arriving by mail as checks or debit cards.

2021-03-15.abstract: The Communist Party has made great strides in reducing China’s pollution, but a perfect storm of northern winds and an industrial rebound has created dangerously high levels of pollution countrywide.

2021-03-15.abstract: The protests in at least 40 cities represented an outpouring of anger over the treatment of women at the highest levels of power, organizers said.

2021-03-15.title: U.K. Policing Bill Examined After Clashes at Sarah Everard Vigil
2021-03-15.abstract: The police’s actions at an event in memory of a woman who was kidnapped and killed in London have put the proposed legislation under a spotlight.

2021-03-16.abstract: With Covid-19 deaths rising to their highest levels yet and a dangerous new virus variant stalking Brazil, the nation’s communications minister went to Beijing, where he met with Huawei executives and made an unusual request.

2021-03-17.title: On vaccines, Trump tells his hesitant supporters, ‘I would recommend it.’
2021-03-17.abstract: A third of Republicans said in a recent poll that they would not be vaccinated, compared with 10 percent of Democrats, and another 20 percent of Republicans said they were unsure.

2021-03-17.title: China approves a fifth Covid-19 vaccine.
2021-03-17.abstract: The vaccine has several drawbacks. One is that it does not produce many T-cells, which help clear infected cells and prevent them from spreading.

2021-03-17.abstract: Tashnuva Anan Shishir’s triumphant moment came after a long fight for equality that included harassment, bullying and suicide attempts.

2021-03-17.abstract: Setting a confrontational tone ahead of meetings in Alaska, the United States punished Chinese officials involved in eroding democracy in Hong Kong.

2021-03-18.abstract: Ursula von der Leyen, president of the European Commission, accused AstraZeneca of underdelivering coronavirus vaccines, and said the E.U. was ready to use “whatever tool” to ensure it gets “its fair share.”

2021-03-18.abstract: Washington’s recent attempts to communicate with Pyongyang were rebuffed, leaving American officials to appeal to countries in the region to help pressure North Korea.

2021-03-18.title: Tokyo Olympics Official Resigns After Calling Plus-Size Celebrity ‘Olympig’
2021-03-18.abstract: The departure came just a month after the president of the Tokyo organizing committee stepped down over a sexist remark.

2021-03-18.abstract: The Green party mayor of Lyon, a gastronomic capital, introduced no-meat menus in schools. Let the anguish begin.

2021-03-18.title: Canadians to Stand Trial in China for Spying: What We Know
2021-03-18.abstract: Secrecy and politics will likely hang over the cases involving two Canadian men, who have been held largely in isolation for more than two years.

2021-03-19.title: 13 Law Enforcement Officers Killed in Mexico Ambush
2021-03-19.abstract: The attack appeared to be the deadliest assault on the Mexican police in well over a year, illustrating the severe security challenges facing the government.

2021-03-19.title: Women Posing in Children’s Clothing? Fad Sparks Body-Shaming Concerns.
2021-03-19.abstract: It’s the latest viral challenge to sweep Chinese social media, igniting worries that it reinforces the country’s narrow beauty norms.

2021-03-19.abstract: Malaysia’s agreement to extradite the man is a victory in Washington’s efforts to crack down on what it has called sanctions-evading activities by North Koreans.

2021-03-19.abstract: Accounts of atrocities keep coming in as the wounded flee to the regional capital, Mekelle, where Tigrayans say they are being winnowed for their leaders’ rebellion.

2021-03-20.abstract: The club is set to play host to events during the Republican National Committee spring retreat in Florida next month. It has been Mr. Trump’s post-presidency base of operations.

2021-03-20.abstract: On Friday, governments across Europe raced to restart use of AstraZeneca’s coronavirus vaccine after the announcement from the European Union’s top drug regulator on Thursday that the shots were safe and effective.

2021-03-20.title: Where Europe Went Wrong in Its Vaccine Rollout, and Why
2021-03-20.abstract: While Washington went into business with the drug companies, Europe was more fiscally conservative and trusted the free market.

2021-03-20.title: A Volcano Erupted in Iceland. Björk Was ‘Sooo Excited.’
2021-03-20.abstract: No injuries were reported after the rare eruption near Reykjavik — only joy, on the part of the singer, volcanologists and other Icelanders.

2021-03-20.title: Palestinian Hamlet Embodies Fight for the West Bank’s Future
2021-03-20.abstract: Israel suspended a decision to annex a section of the territory last year, but its military still exerts tight control over Palestinians in the area.

2021-03-21.title: New York reports its first case of the troubling variant that is dominant in Brazil.
2021-03-21.abstract: Health officials worry that variants will become the dominant source of infection soon as thousands of cases continue to be reported for other variants found in Britain, South Africa and Brazil.

2021-03-21.title: India Battles a Second Covid-19 Wave and Vaccine Skepticism
2021-03-21.abstract: An outbreak around Mumbai has led to a campaign for more inoculations. That could affect supplies around the world.

2021-03-21.abstract: Despite warnings, American and European officials gave up leverage that could have guaranteed access for billions of people. That risks prolonging the pandemic.

2021-03-21.title: A Violent End to a Desperate Dream Leaves a Guatemalan Town Grieving
2021-03-21.abstract: The trek from Central America to U.S. soil has always been perilous, but a massacre with many victims from one corner of Guatemala has shaken that country.

2021-03-21.title: Palestinians and Israelis Both Vote Soon. The Differences Are Stark.
2021-03-21.abstract: Many Israelis feel numbed by their endless election cycle. Many Palestinians are excited about a rare chance to vote — but others expect little change without statehood.

2021-03-22.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-03-22.abstract: Vaccine inequity that threatens us all.

2021-03-22.title: Second Canadian Is Tried in China on Spying Charges
2021-03-22.abstract: Michael Kovrig, a former diplomat, was tried in secret, Canadian officials said. The case seen as retaliation for Canada’s detention of a Chinese executive.

2021-03-22.title: Australia’s Worst Floods in Decades Quicken Concerns About Climate Change
2021-03-22.abstract: In a country that suffered the harshest wildfires in its recorded history just a year ago, the deluge has become another awful milestone.

2021-03-22.abstract: We spent months reviewing ship-tracking data, corporate records and satellite imagery to uncover one way North Korea evades strict international sanctions.

2021-03-22.abstract: The closing of beloved bookstores is the latest in a series of blows to the neighborhood’s cultural vibrancy, a long decline accelerated by the pandemic.

2021-03-23.title: 137 People Killed in Niger in Series of Attacks on Villages Along Mali Border
2021-03-23.abstract: Gunmen on motorcycles struck in coordinated raids on villages on Sunday in southwestern Niger, a country being ravaged by Islamist violence.

2021-03-23.title: Germany introduces a strict, five-day lockdown over Easter, and asks citizens to stay home.
2021-03-23.abstract: Existing restrictions will be extended until April 18 to stop rising coronavirus cases.

2021-03-23.title: Australia Experiences Worst Flooding In Decades
2021-03-23.abstract: After days of unrelenting rain, major flooding in eastern Australia forced nearly 20,000 people to be evacuated and more than 150 schools to be closed.

2021-03-23.abstract: Britain’s policing and crime bill has set off a string of demonstrations in cities around the country. So what is the proposed legislation and why are people protesting against it?

2021-03-23.abstract: In Britain and beyond, protesters on the left and right are rebelling against virus restrictions, drawing harsh police responses — and questions about the officers’ legitimacy.

2021-03-24.title: E.U. Will Curb Covid Vaccine Exports for 6 Weeks
2021-03-24.abstract: The European Union presented new emergency rules that will most likely severely cut exports to Britain and other countries to ease supply shortages at home.

2021-03-24.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s party took a lead in Israel’s fourth election in two years, but updated exit polls projected a stalemate that could extend Israel’s political deadlock.

2021-03-24.abstract: The Bangladesh authorities say that at least 15 people have died after a devastating fire ripped through the Rohingya refugee camp in Cox’s Bazar, leaving hundreds injured and tens of thousands homeless once again.

2021-03-24.abstract: New satellite images obtained by The New York Times show that China has allowed tankers to use its infrastructure and territorial waters to deliver oil to North Korea.

2021-03-25.title: Shadowed by Pandemic, Olympic Torch Relay Begins in Japan
2021-03-25.abstract: The ceremony kicked off in Fukushima Prefecture, a heavily symbolic choice a decade after the tsunami and nuclear disaster there.

2021-03-25.title: ‘Power for Power’: North Korea Returns to a Show of Force
2021-03-25.abstract: Pyongyang’s recent ballistic missiles test indicated that the country is once again raising tensions to gain leverage with Washington.

2021-03-25.title: Supply Isn’t the Only Thing Stymying Europe’s Vaccine Rollout
2021-03-25.abstract: Bureaucratic inertia, a diffusion of responsibility and logistical problems have seriously undercut vaccination efforts. In Italy, it’s the older population that bears the brunt.

2021-03-25.title: New Zealand Approves Paid Leave After Miscarriage
2021-03-25.abstract: The measure, believed to be among the first in the world, would apply to couples who lose a pregnancy at any point.

2021-03-26.title: Australia Is Returning to Normal. But Should Everything?
2021-03-26.abstract: From JobSeeker to working from home to … online menus?

2021-03-26.title: Israel’s Shadow War With Iran Moves Out to Sea
2021-03-26.abstract: Israel and Iran have fought a clandestine war across the Middle East for years, mainly by land and air. Now ships are under attack in the Mediterranean and Red Seas.

2021-03-26.title: North Korea’s Arsenal Has Grown Rapidly. Here’s What’s in It.
2021-03-26.abstract: The country has one of the largest standing armies in the world, but much of its equipment is old and obsolete. North Korea has sought to make up for those shortcomings by building nuclear weapons.

2021-03-26.title: What the people cleaning New York City’s subway want passengers to know.
2021-03-26.abstract: Cleaning the New York City subway has always been a dirty job. But when the pandemic hit last spring, it became even more challenging. When Gov. Andrew M. Cuomo ordered that trains be shut down overnight for cleaning, the Metropolitan Transportation Authority turned to contractors to help undertake the monumental task of scouring the trains in the nation’s largest transit system.

2021-03-27.title: The E.U. approves more vaccine production sites amid lagging inoculation efforts.
2021-03-27.abstract: A top European drug agency gave a green light to new plants in the Netherlands, Germany and Switzerland for AstraZeneca, Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna vaccines.

2021-03-27.title: Modi’s Visit to Bangladesh Sets Off Violent Protests
2021-03-27.abstract: At least four people were killed in clashes between the police and demonstrators, who have denounced Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina for inviting India’s leader.

2021-03-27.title: Children Trapped by Colombia’s War, Five Years After Peace Deal
2021-03-27.abstract: Despite a 2016 peace deal with the FARC, Colombia’s long internal conflict continues. Seldom has that been as evident as this month, when the government bombed a rebel camp full of young people.

2021-03-27.title: Skiing in Himalayas Is ‘Like a Beautiful Dream,’ Despite Conflict and Coronavirus
2021-03-27.abstract: A Kashmir resort is welcoming the highest number of skiers in decades as Indians eager to get outdoors after lockdowns flock there even amid a simmering separatist movement and a security clampdown.

2021-03-27.title: A Collapse Foretold: How Brazil’s Covid-19 Outbreak Overwhelmed Hospitals
2021-03-27.abstract: The virus has killed more than 300,000 people in Brazil, its spread aided by a highly contagious variant, political infighting and distrust of science.

2021-03-28.title: Many U.S. graduations are curtailed again, but some commencements will go on without guests.
2021-03-28.abstract: Many universities stipulate that to participate in graduation, students must test negative for the coronavirus beforehand and have a record of adhering to campus restrictions.

2021-03-28.title: Explosion Rocks Indonesian Church Compound on Palm Sunday
2021-03-28.abstract: No churchgoers were killed, but at least 19 people were injured, the police said. President Joko Widodo called it an act of terrorism.

2021-03-28.title: His Plane Crashed in the Amazon. Then Came the Hard Part.
2021-03-28.abstract: A Brazilian pilot working for wildcat miners escaped death when his plane went down in a remote area. He walked through the jungle for 36 days before being rescued.

2021-03-28.title: Syria says it will ration fuel as the economic toll of the blockage grows.
2021-03-28.abstract: The government of Syria has said that it will begin rationing the use of fuel after the closure of the Suez Canal delayed the delivery of a critical shipment of oil to the war-torn nation.

2021-03-28.title: Russia Trumpets Vaccine Exports, While Quietly Importing Doses
2021-03-28.abstract: Russia has held up the Sputnik vaccine as a triumph of its scientists. But the country’s inoculation program still relies on cross-border trade.

2021-03-29.title: Oil Refinery in Indonesia Catches Fire, Prompting an Evacuation
2021-03-29.abstract: The state-owned oil company, Pertamina, which operates the refinery on the island of Java, suggested the fire may have been caused by a lightning strike.

2021-03-29.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-03-29.abstract: The Ever Given is partially freed.

2021-03-29.title: ‘We pulled it off!’ After days of arduous labor, a ship is free, and salvagers are triumphant.
2021-03-29.abstract: The operation that wrested the ship from an embankment where it had beached six days ago was one of the most intense in maritime history. Some Suez Canal traffic resumed within hours.

2021-03-29.title: Amid Accusations of Rape and Misogyny, Australia’s Leader Demotes 2 Ministers
2021-03-29.abstract: The attorney general and the defense minister, tied to separate scandals, will stay in the government, Prime Minister Scott Morrison said.

2021-03-29.title: An Alliance of Autocracies? China Wants to Lead a New World Order.
2021-03-29.abstract: As President Biden predicts a struggle between democracies and their opponents, Beijing is eager to champion the other side.

2021-03-30.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-03-30.abstract: The first day of the murder trial of Derek Chauvin.

2021-03-30.title: World leaders call for an international treaty to combat future pandemics.
2021-03-30.abstract: A letter published in newspapers around the globe pressed for a cross-border understanding, similar to the one that followed World War II, to prepare for widespread health emergencies.

2021-03-30.title: The Taliban Think They Have Already Won, Peace Deal or Not
2021-03-30.abstract: “We have defeated the enemy.” The international community is scrambling to secure peace in Afghanistan, but the Taliban believe they have the upper hand — and are saying as much.

2021-03-30.title: As mask mandates lift, retail workers again feel vulnerable.
2021-03-30.abstract: Many retail and fast-food workers in states like Mississippi and Texas, where governments have removed mask mandates before a majority of people have been vaccinated and while troubling new variants of the coronavirus are appearing, are feeling a heightened anxiety.

2021-03-30.title: How China Plans to Control Hong Kong’s Elections
2021-03-30.abstract: New rules give Chinese security bodies power to investigate all potential candidates, meaning that opposition politicians face steep odds of even being allowed to run.

2021-03-31.title: Your Wednesday Briefing
2021-03-31.abstract: Surging coronavirus cases in the U.S.

2021-03-31.title: A Terrifying Disease Stalks Seaside Australia: Flesh-Eating Ulcers
2021-03-31.abstract: As Buruli ulcer cases have risen, they have taken a physical and psychological toll but also offered hope that scientists can solve the bacteria’s many mysteries.

2021-03-31.title: Cockpit Recorder Unit From Indonesian Crash Is Finally Recovered
2021-03-31.abstract: After months of searching, Indonesian officials say they have found the crucial memory unit from Sriwijaya Air Flight 182. Investigators hope it will help explain why the plane crashed in January.

2021-03-31.title: See How Rich Countries Got to the Front of the Vaccine Line
2021-03-31.abstract: Many low-income nations lacked the means to preorder doses, and their residents have received just .1 percent of vaccine shots given worldwide.

2021-03-31.title: Building a Mosque in France, Never Easy, May Get Even Harder
2021-03-31.abstract: The country’s principle of secularism has long disadvantaged Muslim communities, experts say, and President Emmanuel Macron’s new law against Islamism could deepen the disparities.
